title: A small lie I told as a child
summary: Narrate a moment when you told a small lie as a child and what happened next.
topic: A small lie I told as a child
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small lie you told when you were a child. Describe the situation, what you said, and why you said it. Use past simple and past continuous. Explain what happened after the lie and how you felt about it.
hintStarters: When I was about … years old, I told a small lie that I still remember…
One afternoon at school, I said something that was not true because…
I was sitting in the classroom when my teacher asked me…
sampleText: When I was nine years old, I told a small lie at my primary school in Toronto that I still think about sometimes. My teacher asked if I had finished my homework assignment, and I said yes, even though I had not started it. I was sitting at my desk and all my classmates were watching, so I was too embarrassed to tell the truth. I thought I could finish the work at lunchtime and bring it to her later. At lunch, I was playing in the yard with my friends and I forgot all about the homework. The next morning, my teacher asked me to hand it in. I had nothing to give her. She looked at me quietly and waited. I felt my face go red and my stomach hurt. I finally told her the truth. She did not shout at me. She just said that one small lie always makes more problems than the truth does. She gave me extra time to finish the work, and I stayed inside at break to do it. I felt ashamed for the rest of that day. But I also felt better after I confessed, because carrying a lie is more tiring than telling the truth.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: even though I had not started it
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mặc dù tôi chưa bắt đầu làm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I was sitting at my desk
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang ngồi tại bàn của mình

sampleAnnotations.phrase: too embarrassed to tell the truth
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: quá xấu hổ để nói thật

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I forgot all about the homework
asked me to hand it in
I felt my face go red
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi cảm thấy mặt mình đỏ lên

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous for setting the scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past continuous để miêu tả những gì đang xảy ra xung quanh khi câu chuyện diễn ra: 'I was sitting at my desk and all my classmates were watching.' Các hành động chính dùng past simple.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past continuous to describe what was happening around you when the story takes place: 'I was sitting at my desk and all my classmates were watching.' Use past simple for the main actions.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi chín tuổi, tôi đã nói một lời nói dối nhỏ ở trường tiểu học tại Toronto mà đôi khi tôi vẫn nghĩ đến. Cô giáo hỏi tôi đã hoàn thành bài tập về nhà chưa, và tôi nói rồi, dù tôi chưa bắt đầu làm. Tôi đang ngồi tại bàn và tất cả bạn cùng lớp đang nhìn, nên tôi quá xấu hổ để nói thật. Tôi nghĩ mình có thể làm bài vào giờ ăn trưa và mang nộp cho cô sau. Lúc ăn trưa, tôi đang chơi ngoài sân với bạn bè và quên mất bài tập. Sáng hôm sau, cô giáo yêu cầu tôi nộp bài. Tôi không có gì để nộp. Cô nhìn tôi im lặng và chờ đợi. Tôi cảm thấy mặt mình đỏ lên và bụng đau. Cuối cùng tôi nói thật với cô. Cô không mắng tôi. Cô chỉ nói rằng một lời nói dối nhỏ luôn tạo ra nhiều rắc rối hơn là nói thật. Cô cho tôi thêm thời gian để làm bài và tôi ở lại trong lớp vào giờ ra chơi để hoàn thành. Tôi cảm thấy xấu hổ suốt cả ngày hôm đó. Nhưng tôi cũng cảm thấy nhẹ nhõm hơn sau khi thú nhận, vì mang một lời nói dối mệt mỏi hơn là nói thật.

mcQuestions.prompt: What lie did the writer tell?
options: That a friend had taken the homework
That the teacher had not set homework
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer said 'yes' when asked if the homework was done, even though it was not.

options: Because classmates were watching and the writer felt embarrassed
Because the writer did not like the teacher
Because the writer wanted more time
Because the homework was too difficult
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer says: 'I was too embarrassed to tell the truth.'
mcQuestions.hint: What feeling made the writer say the wrong thing?

options: The writer forgot about the homework while playing
The writer finished the homework quickly
The writer asked a friend for help
The writer told the teacher the truth
mcQuestions.explanation: The writer was playing in the yard and forgot all about the homework.
mcQuestions.hint: What was the writer doing at lunch?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past continuous for a background scene?
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'were watching' describes what classmates were doing in the background.
mcQuestions.hint: Background actions in a past story use past continuous.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I felt my face ___ red.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Feel + object + base verb' is the correct pattern: 'I felt my face go red.'
mcQuestions.hint: After 'feel + object', use the base form of the verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: What did the teacher do after the writer told the truth?
options: Gave extra time to finish the work
mcQuestions.explanation: The teacher gave extra time and the writer stayed inside at break to finish.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for what the teacher offered after the confession.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'to admit something wrong you did'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Confess' means to admit that you did something wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: The writer used this word near the end of the passage.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which linker shows contrast in this sentence: '___ I had not started it, I said yes.'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even though' introduces a contrast — the writer said yes despite not having started.
mcQuestions.hint: Which linker means 'despite the fact that'?

options: Give the homework to the teacher
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Hand in' means to give a piece of work to a teacher or person in authority.
mcQuestions.hint: What does a student do with finished work at school?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly expresses feeling better after telling the truth?
options: I felt better after I confessed.
I felt better since I confessed.
I was felt better after I confessed.
I feel better after I confessed.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'felt' is correct for a completed past experience.
mcQuestions.hint: This is a past event, so use past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct use of 'too + adjective + to + verb'.
options: I was too embarrassed to tell the truth.
I was too embarrassed for tell the truth.
I was too embarrassed telling the truth.
I was too embarrassed that I tell the truth.
mcQuestions.explanation: The pattern is 'too + adjective + to + base verb': too embarrassed to tell.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Too ... to ...' means so much of something that you cannot do the next thing.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do children sometimes lie to avoid trouble? Do you think small lies can cause big problems? What is the best way to help children learn to tell the truth?
